canonical_definition: Based on the provided literature, Representativeness is a construct defined as the degree to which a subset of training data captures the essential information from the full dataset, reflecting both sufficiency and necessity. This concept is framed in the context of selecting a "core subset of demonstrations" from a larger training set. The stated goals for creating such a representative subset are to maintain data quality while also minimizing computational expenses, resulting in what one paper calls a "faithful representation" that is as minimal in size as possible. In terms of measurement, the construct is operationalized in one study through the use of the OpinionQA instrument.
papers: Large Language Models are Demonstration Pre-Selectors for Themselves
Position: Challenges and Future Directions of Data-Centric AI Alignment
Position: Human Baselines in Model Evaluations Need Rigor and Transparency (With Recommendations & Reporting Checklist)

definitions.text: The degree to which a subset of training data captures the essential information needed for a model to perform well across tasks, reflecting both sufficiency and necessity with respect to the full dataset.
definitions.paper: Large Language Models are Demonstration Pre-Selectors for Themselves

relationships.paper: PersonalLLM: Tailoring LLMs to Individual Preferences

iclr25: PersonalLLM: Tailoring LLMs to Individual Preferences
